---
title: "portfolio"
author: "Arunesh Venkatesan"
format: html
editor: visual
---

Portfolio item 6

```{r}
diabetes_data <- read.csv("/Users/aruneshvenkatesan/Documents/RU_DATA/SEM1/STAT/diabetes.csv")
model <- glm(Outcome ~ Glucose + BMI + Age, data = diabetes_data, family = "binomial")
summary(model)
```

This tells us how much the "odds" of diabetes multiply for every 1-unit increase in the variable.

```{r}
odds_ratios <- exp(coef(model))
print(odds_ratios)
```

item 7

```{r}
# MODEL 1: Generalized Linear Model (Logistic Regression)
glm_model <- glm(Outcome ~ Glucose + BMI + Age, 
                 data = diabetes_data, 
                 family = "binomial")

# MODEL 2: Ordinary Least Squares (Linear Regression)
lm_model <- lm(Outcome ~ Glucose + BMI + Age, 
               data = diabetes_data)

print("--- Model 1 (GLM) Coefficients ---")
# Positive Estimate = Increases Risk
print(coef(summary(glm_model)))

print("--- Model 2 (Least Squares) Coefficients ---")
# Positive Estimate = Increases Score
print(coef(summary(lm_model)))

# Check specific agreement on Glucose (The most important factor)
print("--- Agreement Check: Glucose ---")
print(paste("GLM Glucose Effect:", coef(glm_model)["Glucose"]))
print(paste("LM  Glucose Effect:", coef(lm_model)["Glucose"]))

```

item 8

```{r}

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 1: Fit Two Different Models
# ---------------------------------------------------------

# Model A (The "Simple" Model)
model_simple <- glm(Outcome ~ Glucose, 
                    data = diabetes_data, 
                    family = "binomial")

# Model B (The "Complex" Model)
model_complex <- glm(Outcome ~ Glucose + BMI + Age, 
                     data = diabetes_data, 
                     family = "binomial")

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 2: Compare using Likelihood-based Metrics
# ---------------------------------------------------------

# 1. Compare AIC (Lower is better)
print("--- AIC Comparison (Lower is Better) ---")
print(paste("Simple Model AIC: ", round(model_simple$aic, 2)))
print(paste("Complex Model AIC:", round(model_complex$aic, 2)))

```

item 9

```{r}
if(!require(dagitty)) install.packages("dagitty")
library(dagitty)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 1: Construct the Causal Graph (DAG)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
dag_model <- dagitty("dag {
    Age -> BMI
    Age -> Outcome
    Pregnancies -> BMI
    Pregnancies -> Outcome
    DiabetesPedigreeFunction -> BMI
    DiabetesPedigreeFunction -> Outcome
    
    BMI -> Glucose -> Outcome
    BMI -> BloodPressure -> Outcome
    BMI -> Insulin -> Outcome
    BMI -> SkinThickness -> Outcome
    
    BMI -> Outcome
}")

exposures(dag_model) <- "BMI"
outcomes(dag_model) <- "Outcome"

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# NEW STEP: Plot the DAG Diagram
# ---------------------------------------------------------
plot(dag_model)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 2: Derive the Adjustment Set (For DIRECT Effect)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
adj_set <- adjustmentSets(dag_model, exposure = "BMI", outcome = "Outcome", effect = "direct")

print("--- Required Adjustment Set for Direct Effect ---")
print(adj_set)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 3: Perform Causal Analysis (Direct Effect)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
causal_model <- glm(Outcome ~ BMI + Age + Pregnancies + DiabetesPedigreeFunction + 
                    Glucose + BloodPressure + Insulin + SkinThickness,
                    data = diabetes_data, 
                    family = "binomial")

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 4: Interpret the Result
# ---------------------------------------------------------
print("--- Direct Causal Effect of BMI (Coefficients) ---")
print(summary(causal_model)$coefficients["BMI", ])

pval <- summary(causal_model)$coefficients["BMI", "Pr(>|z|)"]
if(pval < 0.05) {
    print("CONCLUSION: BMI has a DIRECT effect on diabetes, distinct from Glucose/BP.")
} else {
    print("CONCLUSION: BMI acts mostly THROUGH Glucose and other mediators.")
}
```
